meaning: adj.性质相反的,矛盾的,不调和的
examples: Those two blood groups are incompatible.
These two objectives are mutually incompatible.
This made its mini-computers incompatible with its mainframes.
Some frequent travelers carry multiple phones to navigate incompatible cellular standards country to country.
There are more than a dozen incompatible yet fundamentally correct ways to measure time: Earth-centered?
